propositions: release.yml references first-party actions by mutable major-version tags instead of full commit SHAs, so identical rebar SHAs can execute different build/publish code.
Release checkouts do not set persist-credentials: false, leaving the token persisted in local git config by default.

Build tools install with unrestricted upgrades and Hatchling is unpinned, so the build environment must be hash-locked and installed with --require-hashes under --no-isolation, guarded by a parsed installed-vs-lock comparison.
The MCP publisher is downloaded from a mutable releases/latest URL and executed without digest/signature verification inside a job holding id-token: write.

Enforce co-residency structurally by splitting into an unprivileged mcp_verify job that downloads and verifies the archive and an OIDC-privileged mcp_registry job that consumes only the verified artifact and restores the exec bit.
Adopt zizmor and actionlint under make lint scoped to release.yml, plus rebar-specific unit tests, and wire per-job release-evidence fragments concatenated into release-evidence.txt.
